description: PhD-level marketing knowledge taxonomy — 12 domains, 156 topics, 5 content types, 4 verticals = 3,120 unique generation slots

verticals.label: IBEW 134 Electrical Contractors
verticals.context: union electrical contractors, NEC code compliance, prevailing wage projects, commercial/residential electrical

verticals.label: General Contractors / HVAC / Plumbing
verticals.context: licensed general contractors, HVAC technicians, plumbers, multi-trade firms

verticals.label: SMB SaaS & Professional Services
verticals.context: small business software companies, consultants, agencies, professional service firms

verticals.label: Platform & Marketplace Operators
verticals.context: two-sided marketplaces, contractor platforms, service booking apps

contentTypes.label: Strategic Advisory
contentTypes.prompt: A {vertical} business owner asks for strategic guidance on {topic}. Provide a framework-driven response with KPIs, implementation steps, compliance guardrails, and risk assessment.

contentTypes.label: Campaign Blueprint
contentTypes.prompt: Design a complete campaign for a {vertical} business applying {topic} principles. Include channel selection, budget allocation, timeline, A/B test hypotheses, stop-loss criteria, and expected ROI benchmarks.

contentTypes.label: Diagnostic Analysis
contentTypes.prompt: A {vertical} business is underperforming in the area of {topic}. Provide root cause analysis with evidence-based checklist, industry benchmarks, remediation plan with rollback triggers, and compliance review.

contentTypes.label: Content Artifact
contentTypes.prompt: Create a marketing content piece (blog post, email sequence, ad copy, or case study) for a {vertical} business applying {topic}. Include measurable CTAs, conversion KPIs, and compliance disclaimers.

contentTypes.label: Anti-Pattern Alert
contentTypes.prompt: A {vertical} business is about to make a common mistake related to {topic}. Identify failure signatures, early warning metrics, historical evidence of what goes wrong, risk assessment, and better alternatives with guardrails.

domains.name: Marketing Strategy Foundations
domains.sourceFrameworks: Philip Kotler (Marketing Management), Michael Porter (Competitive Strategy, Five Forces)
topics: Marketing mix (4Ps/7Ps) applied to trade services
STP framework (segmentation-targeting-positioning) for local contractors
Porter's Five Forces analysis for electrical contracting market
Competitive advantage through differentiation vs cost leadership
Value chain analysis for service businesses
Customer lifetime value modeling for recurring maintenance contracts
Market penetration vs market development strategies for trades
Product-market fit assessment frameworks
Blue ocean strategy applied to underserved trade verticals
Strategic group mapping for local service providers
PESTEL analysis for construction and trades industry
Ansoff matrix decision framework for SMB growth
Resource-based view of sustainable competitive advantage

domains.name: Behavioral Economics & Persuasion
domains.sourceFrameworks: Daniel Kahneman (Prospect Theory, Thinking Fast and Slow), Robert Cialdini (Influence: 6 Principles of Persuasion)
topics: Prospect theory: loss aversion in service pricing and proposals
Anchoring effects in estimate presentation and package pricing
Social proof deployment for contractor credibility and trust
Reciprocity principle in lead nurturing sequences
Scarcity and urgency in seasonal service promotions
Authority building through certification and union credentials
Commitment and consistency in customer retention programs
Choice architecture for service tier selection
Framing effects in proposal win rates
Endowment effect in subscription and retainer pricing
Availability heuristic in safety-focused marketing
Default bias in package structuring and upsells
Peak-end rule in customer experience design

domains.name: Brand Strategy & Positioning
domains.sourceFrameworks: Al Ries & Jack Trout (Positioning), David Ogilvy (Advertising Principles), Byron Sharp (How Brands Grow)
topics: Positioning theory: owning a category in local markets
Brand salience and mental availability (Byron Sharp)
Physical availability and distribution for service businesses
Distinctive brand assets for trades companies
Category entry points for electrical HVAC plumbing services

Long copy vs short copy decisions by awareness level
Brand architecture for multi-service contractors
Rebranding frameworks and transition playbooks
Employer branding for skilled trades recruitment
Community brand building through union partnerships

Brand measurement: awareness consideration preference tracking

topics: Product-led growth frameworks adapted for service businesses

Message-market match by awareness level

Customer language mining for authentic copy

domains.sourceFrameworks: Marketing failure analysis, compliance violations, wasted spend patterns

Price war initiation and race-to-bottom dynamics
Compliance violations in advertising claims

Brand inconsistency across channels and touchpoints
Customer acquisition without retention planning
Over-automation: removing human touch from service delivery

Marketing-sales disconnect and lead handoff failures
